events.title: Avignon Papacy begins
events.description: Popes reside in Avignon rather than Rome, increasing tensions over papal authority and European politics.

events.title: Arrest of the Knights Templar
events.description: Philip IV of France orders the arrest of Templar leaders; the order is later dissolved by the papacy.

events.title: Ottomans capture Bursa
events.description: Ottoman forces capture Bursa, establishing an early Ottoman capital and base for expansion in Anatolia.

events.title: Battle of Bannockburn
events.description: Scottish forces under Robert the Bruce defeat the English, securing Scottish independence for a time.

events.title: Great Famine in Europe
events.description: Widespread crop failures and food shortages across Europe cause high mortality and social stress.

events.title: Mansa Musa pilgrimage to Mecca
events.description: Mali's Mansa Musa undertakes a famous hajj, displaying immense wealth and boosting Mali's reputation across the Islamic world.

events.title: Vijayanagara Empire founded
events.description: Harihara I and Bukka Raya establish the Vijayanagara Empire in South India, promoting Hindu culture and resisting northern invasions.

events.title: Hundred Years' War begins
events.description: Protracted conflict between England and France over dynastic claims and territorial control in France.

events.title: Battle of Crécy
events.description: English forces win a decisive victory over the French, demonstrating the effectiveness of longbowmen in medieval warfare.

events.title: Black Death pandemic
events.description: Bubonic plague spreads across Eurasia, causing massive mortality and profound social, economic, and cultural changes.

events.title: Battle of Poitiers
events.description: English forces capture the French king John II, a major English victory during the Hundred Years' War.

events.title: Ming dynasty established
events.description: Zhu Yuanzhang (Hongwu) overthrows the Yuan dynasty and establishes the Ming, restoring Han Chinese rule and reorganizing the state.

events.title: Timur (Tamerlane) begins campaigns
events.description: Timur establishes a Central Asian empire through military campaigns across West, Central, and South Asia, noted for both destruction and patronage of arts.

events.title: Peasant unrest and revolts
events.description: Economic stress and social change after the Black Death lead to uprisings and unrest across Europe, including early precursors to larger revolts.

events.title: Battle of Kulikovo
events.description: Dmitry Donskoy of Moscow defeats the Mongol-Tatar forces at Kulikovo, a symbolic step toward Muscovite independence from Mongol rule.

events.title: Peasants' Revolt in England
events.description: Widespread popular uprising against taxation and social inequality; briefly threatens royal authority before suppression.

events.title: Battle of Kosovo
events.description: Clash between Ottoman forces and a Balkan coalition; the battle has long-term significance for Ottoman expansion into the Balkans.

events.title: Timur defeats Ottoman forces at Ankara (Anatolia campaign)
events.description: (Note: campaign culminates in 1402) Timur's campaigns into Anatolia culminate in the defeat of Ottoman Sultan Bayezid I at Ankara, temporarily disrupting Ottoman expansion.

events.title: Muromachi (Ashikaga) shogunate begins
events.description: Ashikaga Takauji establishes the Muromachi shogunate, marking a new era of samurai governance and cultural developments in Japan.

events.title: Angkor environmental and political stresses
events.description: Angkor faces environmental, hydraulic, and political challenges that contribute to gradual decline in Khmer central power.
